name: El Patron
content:
A nice Mexican grocery store within walking distance of the university. It is a nice walk, if it weren’t for all of the cars on Holmes Avenue. If you go early enough on a Sunday morning, you can get some peace on the walk The walk has gotten a little more enjoyable since they repaved Holmes Ave, which made the cars a little quieter, but at the end of the day the only real solution is to get rid of the cars. Oh and also get rid of Jeeps and Mall Haulers with off road tires in the city. Those things are a nightmare when it comes to disturbing the peace.. The staff is still unsure as to whether or not I speak Spanish, thanks to my ability to convincingly mumble my way through every conversation despite only speaking two words of Spanish.
People at the university keep on saying that the university is located right next to “the hood” while motioning in the general direction of the neighborhood where El Patron is located. The neighborhood itself is actually quite nice, with people going for evening strolls and washing their cars trucks in their driveways, and El Paton itself is also a respectable location. I am convinced that people at my university think El Patron is in “the hood” because it is a Mexican grocery store.
There is a taqueria in the back of the store, though I have never been there myself.
